description: Feathers, wings, and a beak place an animal under 'pelori', the bird category. Many birds fly; penguins and ostriches make sure flight never becomes the admission test. 'nolika' (animal) is wider, while a local or scientific name can identify the species.
articulatory_notes: A brief unvoiced lip closure releases into front 'e'. Stressed lateral 'lo' brings the tongue to the alveolar ridge; a tap or brief trill then leads into high-front 'i'.
usage_notes: Counts allow optional 'lipha'. Registered compounds include 'nuko pelori' (blackbird) and 'pelori womu' (bird nest or home).

examples.translation: A bird sings in the tree.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: a living source of observed song, colour, and flight
semantic_domains.nature: a feathered winged vertebrate, whether or not it flies
